label: Positioning Statement
description: Craft a sharp market positioning statement for a brand.

fields.label: Brand / product name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: What it is & does
fields.placeholder: An expense tool that auto-categorizes spend for small teams.

fields.label: Main alternatives
fields.placeholder: Spreadsheets, Expensify, doing nothing.

fields.label: Key differentiator
fields.placeholder: Zero-setup categorization that actually learns.

fields.label: Market category
fields.placeholder: Spend management for small teams

fields.label: Proof points / evidence
fields.placeholder: Saves 6 hrs/month; 4.8 rating; SOC 2.

template:
You are a brand strategist. Craft a positioning statement for "{{brandName}}".

## The offering
{{offering}}

## Audience
Target: {{audience}}.

## Competitive context
Alternatives: {{competitors}}
Key differentiator: {{differentiator}}
{{#if category}}Category we play in: {{category}}.
{{/if}}{{#if proofPoints}}Proof: {{proofPoints}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Deliver a tight classic positioning statement (for [audience] who [need], [brand] is the [category] that [benefit] because [reason to believe]).
- Make the differentiator unmistakable and the benefit emotionally resonant, not feature-led.
- Ensure it is defensible against the listed alternatives.
{{#if frameworks}}- Provide 2 alternative framings (e.g. challenger vs category-leader) and when to use each.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The primary positioning statement.
2. A one-line short version for internal use.
3. The strategic rationale behind the chosen angle.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Brand Naming
description: Generate and evaluate brand or product name candidates.

fields.label: What you're naming
fields.placeholder: A privacy-first password manager for families.

fields.label: Desired personality
fields.placeholder: Trustworthy, calm, a little playful

fields.label: Words / associations to avoid
fields.placeholder: Nothing techy-sounding; avoid 'secure/safe'.

template:
You are an expert brand namer. Generate names for the following.

## What we're naming
{{whatItIs}}

## Direction
- Audience: {{audience}}
- Personality: {{personality}}
- Styles to explore: {{styles}}
{{#if avoid}}- Avoid: {{avoid}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Produce {{count}} distinct candidates spread across the requested styles.
- For each: the name, a one-line rationale, pronunciation if non-obvious, and tone fit.
- Flag obvious risks (generic, hard to spell, negative connotations).
{{#if domainCheck}}- Suggest realistic domain/handle strategies (exact, modifier, or alt TLD).
{{/if}}- Then shortlist the top 3 with the reasoning for the ranking.

## Deliverables
1. The full candidate list with rationale.
2. A top-3 shortlist and why.
3. Recommended next validation steps (trademark, linguistic, audience test).

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Tagline Set
description: Write a set of taglines and slogans for a brand.

fields.label: Brand name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Brand essence & core promise
fields.placeholder: Effortless money clarity for busy small teams.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Under 5 words; works as an app store subtitle.

template:
You are a senior copywriter. Write taglines for "{{brandName}}".

## Brand essence
{{essence}}

## Direction
- Audience: {{audience}}
- Tone: {{tone}}
- Creative directions: {{directions}}

## Requirements
- {{count}} taglines per creative direction, each distinct and non-generic.
- Tight, memorable, and true to the core promise; no empty superlatives.
- For each, a one-line note on the angle and where it works best.
{{#if constraints}}- Constraints: {{constraints}}.
{{/if}}- Then pick a single recommended hero tagline and justify it.

## Deliverables
1. The full set grouped by direction.
2. The recommended hero tagline with rationale.
3. Two backup options for different channels.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Voice & Tone Guide
description: Define a brand voice and tone guide writers can apply.

fields.label: Brand name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Brand personality
fields.placeholder: Confident expert who is warm and never condescending.

fields.label: Voice principles to anchor on
fields.placeholder: Clear over clever; human; specific; honest.

template:
You are a brand voice strategist. Create a voice and tone guide for "{{brandName}}".

## Personality
{{personality}}

## Context
- Primary audience: {{audience}}
- Baseline tone: {{baseTone}}
{{#if principles}}- Voice principles to build on: {{principles}}.
{{/if}}
## The guide must include
- A clear voice definition: who the brand sounds like and how, in plain terms.
- 3-5 voice principles, each with a short explanation.
- Concrete vocabulary, grammar, and formatting conventions (what we say / avoid).
- Channel-specific guidance for: {{channels}}.
{{#if toneShifts}}- How tone flexes by situation (e.g. errors, celebrations, sensitive moments).
{{/if}}{{#if examples}}- Before/after rewrite examples that show the voice in action.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The complete, usable voice and tone guide.
2. A one-page quick-reference cheat sheet.
3. Notes on rollout and how to keep it consistent.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Brand Story
description: Write a compelling origin and purpose narrative for a brand.

fields.label: Brand name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Origin & founding insight
fields.placeholder: Founders kept losing money to surprise fees and built the fix.

fields.label: Mission & belief
fields.placeholder: Money clarity should be the default, not a luxury.

fields.label: Milestones / proof to weave in
fields.placeholder: 10k customers, bootstrapped, first to do X.

label: Messaging Framework
description: Build a structured messaging framework with pillars and proof.

fields.label: Brand / product name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: What it does
fields.placeholder: Automated spend management for small teams.

fields.label: Audience segments
fields.placeholder: Founders, ops leads, finance-curious solo owners.

fields.label: Core value proposition
fields.placeholder: See and control every dollar without spreadsheets.

fields.label: Candidate message pillars
fields.placeholder: Effortless setup, real visibility, built-in control.

fields.label: Common objections to counter
fields.placeholder: 'Too small to need this', 'switching is painful'.

template:
You are a product marketing strategist. Build a messaging framework for "{{brandName}}".

## Offering
{{offering}}

## Audience segments
{{audiences}}

## Core value proposition
{{valueProp}}
{{#if pillars}}Candidate pillars to refine: {{pillars}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- A clear hierarchy: overarching value prop -> 3-4 message pillars -> proof points.
- Each pillar: a headline message, supporting points, and concrete evidence.
- Tone: {{tone}}; consistent, benefit-led, and free of jargon.
{{#if perSegment}}- Tailored angle and emphasis per audience segment.
{{/if}}{{#if objections}}- Reframes/responses to these objections: {{objections}}.
{{/if}}- A house of messages that scales across channels without contradiction.

## Deliverables
1. The full messaging framework (value prop, pillars, proof).
2. {{#if perSegment}}A per-segment messaging matrix.{{else}}A channel-application cheat sheet.{{/if}}
3. Example headlines/blurbs derived from the framework.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Elevator Pitch
description: Write tight elevator pitches for different contexts.

fields.label: Brand / company name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: What it is & the problem solved
fields.placeholder: Spend management that kills spreadsheet busywork for small teams.

fields.label: Traction / credibility
fields.placeholder: 10k users, 20% MoM growth, bootstrapped.

template:
You are a pitch coach and copywriter. Write elevator pitches for "{{brandName}}".

## What it is
{{whatItIs}}

## Context
- Pitching to: {{audience}}
- Goal: {{goal}}
- Tone: {{tone}}
{{#if traction}}- Credibility to include: {{traction}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Produce these versions: {{lengths}}.
- Lead with the problem and the change you create, not features or jargon.
- Make it concrete, specific, and easy to repeat verbatim.
{{#if includeAsk}}- End each with a single, clear ask aligned to the goal.
{{/if}}- The one-liner must work standalone in a bio or intro.

## Deliverables
1. Each requested pitch version.
2. The recommended default version for this context.
3. Delivery tips (emphasis, pacing, what to cut if rushed).

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Value Proposition
description: Sharpen a customer-centric value proposition and variants.

fields.label: Brand / product name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Job to be done
fields.placeholder: Stay on top of team spending without becoming an accountant.

fields.label: Pains it removes
fields.placeholder: Manual tracking, surprise overspend, month-end scramble.

fields.label: Gains it creates
fields.placeholder: Real-time clarity, calm, hours saved.

fields.label: Why us over alternatives
fields.placeholder: Zero-setup automation that actually learns.

label: Brand Architecture
description: Define how a portfolio of brands and products relate.

fields.label: Company / parent brand
fields.placeholder: Acme Inc.

fields.label: Products / sub-brands
fields.placeholder: Acme Pay, Acme Cards, Acme for Teams, Labs experiments.

fields.label: Current situation & tension
fields.placeholder: Confusing naming; unclear how products relate to the master brand.

fields.label: Distinct audiences served
fields.placeholder: Consumers, SMBs, developers.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Keep flagship equity; minimize customer confusion.

label: Rebrand Brief
description: Write a strategic brief to guide a brand refresh or rebrand.

fields.label: Current brand name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Why rebrand now
fields.placeholder: Outgrown a niche identity; new audience and product scope.

fields.label: Equity to preserve
fields.placeholder: Trusted reputation, recognizable color, loyal core users.

fields.label: Risks & sensitivities
fields.placeholder: Confusing existing customers; SEO/domain impact.
